fields.description:
CI drift gate: every `external: true` volume declared in `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` must be provisioned by `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh`, enforced in `make config-check` (already wired into test.yml AND gerrit-verify.yaml), so a compose-file edit that adds an external volume without a matching provisioning change can never reach `main`. This is the shift-left gate for incident 2731 (commit 10837ca88 added `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets: external: true` without updating compose-up.sh; every `compose up` then failed "external volume not found" for 41h).

## Dependencies
Gated on bug e991-e347-3444-427c (compose-up.sh provisioning fix) landing FIRST: until that fix lands, this check correctly reports the live drift and would fail CI. Land as a stacked change directly on top of e991's commit (declared `depends_on` edge in rebar), so in-session verification runs against a tree where e991's fix is present.

## What
- `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` gains a side-effect-free `--print-volumes` mode: prints the exact external volume names it would create (one per line) and exits BEFORE any dnf/systemctl/docker side effects. The provisioning list itself is the single source of truth — no second copy of the list to drift.
- `infra/scripts/config-check.sh` gains check 5: parse the `external: true` volume names out of docker-compose.yml with `python3` + `yaml.safe_load` (the SAME dependency check 3 already uses — no grep/awk heuristics over multi-line YAML), collect `compose-up.sh --print-volumes` output, and fail (non-zero, named volumes in the message) if any declared external volume is not provisioned.
- Self-test (pattern-regression guard): the check also runs itself against an inline synthetic compose snippet containing a deliberately-unprovisioned external volume and asserts it DETECTS it — a silent false-negative regression of the extraction logic fails CI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1 (red): a synthetic compose fixture declaring external volume `gerrit_notprovisioned: external: true` makes the check exit non-zero naming `gerrit_notprovisioned` — proven by the self-test embedded in check 5 (runs on every `make config-check`).
- [ ] AC2 (green, in-session): `make config-check` exits 0 on this change's tree (stacked on e991's fix, so `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` is provisioned) — proven by running `make config-check` in the worktree before push and by the Verified vote on the stacked change.
- [ ] AC3: `bash infra/scripts/compose-up.sh --print-volumes` prints the full provisioned volume-name list (including `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets`) and performs NO side effects (no docker/dnf/systemctl invocation) — proven by running it on a dev machine without docker privileges.
- [ ] AC4: check 5's failure message names each missing volume and points at compose-up.sh as the fix site.
